conflicts.description: The recurring Burris/Commonwealth rose-claim story changes across chapters: chapter_03 and chapter_08 describe a February seam split with about 6,000 gallons and 17 hybrid teas valued at $4,218.47, while chapter_07 retells it as a September Intex failure with about 15,000 gallons, eleven years of roses, and a rounded $4,200 loss; chapter_14 and chapter_17 introduce further performance-stage drift.
conflicts.canonical_value: Burris/Commonwealth file: February wall failure in Westerville, about 6,000 gallons, 17 hybrid tea roses valued at $4,218.47; later an overlooked umbrella policy pays $1,600.
conflicts.resolution_note: The chapter_03 file review and chapter_08 dinner retelling are anchored to actual claim handling rather than performance, so their specifics are more deeply embedded than the later comic retellings, which visibly mutate under stage pressure.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet's late-chapter availability shorthand drifted: Roy's decisive phone call happens in chapter_16, not chapter_17, Roy is absent from the taping itself, and Layla is remote rather than physically present in chapter_18.
conflicts.canonical_value: Roy is present in chapters 1-3, 9 (phone), 12, 16 (phone), and 18, and absent from the Lincoln Theatre taping; Layla is present in chapters 4, 6, 16, and 17, and only referenced or on phone/text in chapters 12 and 18.
conflicts.resolution_note: Witness and participant presence affects who actually shapes the taping and premiere aftermath, so the manuscript's scene-level appearances override the earlier sheet shorthand.

conflicts.description: Chapter_18 opens with 'Six months later,' but the same chapter also says it is June and ties the return-to-work Monday to the novel's existing early-summer chronology.
conflicts.canonical_value: The special premiere and Doug's return to Mutual Fidelity remain early-summer / June events.
conflicts.resolution_note: The single opener phrase is outweighed by chapter_18's own June-season cues and the rest of the manuscript's four-month February-to-June arc, so the sheet keeps the existing early-summer timing.
